meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Scale Development
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver production-ready software for clients in half the time.
content.answer_block: To escape the 'basic app' trap, you must shift from manual coding to architecture-first development. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you unify authentication, payments, and database schemas instantly. Combine this with Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated logic implementation to ship professional-grade SaaS products with elite speed.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank IDE again, wrestling with the same boilerplate authentication and database schema you built for your last three clients. The 'basic app' trap is a silent career killer; it eats your margins and limits your ability to scale. Every hour spent configuring a new project is an hour you aren't shipping value or increasing your billable rate.

But the landscape has shifted. The elite 1% of freelancers no longer 'code' their foundation—they architect it. By deploying a robust SaaS starter kit, you create a repeatable, high-velocity engine that transforms you from a weary coder into a high-leverage SaaS architect. It is time to stop reinventing the wheel and start building the engines that drive your clients' success.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Stack
steps.description: Establish a consistent foundation using your starter kit to ensure all future projects maintain identical, production-ready standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the enterprise-grade SaaS boilerplate. Configure Next.js 15, Tailwind, and Supabase with Row Level Security enabled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure with standard authentication and database schema from the master boilerplate.'

steps.title: Automating Auth & Payments
steps.description: Never write manual Stripe integration code again. Use the starter kit's pre-built logic to handle subscription tiers and user management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Stripe webhooks and auth middleware. Ensure user role-based access control is mapped to the database schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file stripe-webhooks.ts --prompt 'Implement Stripe subscription lifecycle hooks and connect them to the user profile table.'

steps.title: Component Library Standardisation
steps.description: Standardize your UI components so your clients get a premium, consistent look and feel without custom CSS rework.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Extract the core UI component library into a shared module. Ensure Shadcn/UI integration is optimized for rapid theme switching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file components/ui --prompt 'Refactor components for maximum reusability and consistent design system tokens.'

steps.title: Database Schema Optimization
steps.description: Ensure your database is production-ready by automating migration scripts and indexing strategies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate optimized PostgreSQL migration scripts for the new SaaS project, including proper indexing for user-facing query performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Analyze schema and generate SQL migrations for optimized performance based on the user-subscription model.'

steps.title: Deployment Pipeline Automation
steps.description: Automate the CI/CD process to push updates to production with a single command, minimizing manual deployment errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure GitHub Actions for automated testing and Vercel deployment. Include preview environment triggers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a production-ready CI/CD pipeline configuration for Vercel and GitHub Actions.'

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'Golden Repo' that you pull from; never copy-paste code manually between projects.
Use Claude Code to document your codebase in real-time as you build; clients pay a premium for clean, documented code.
Implement feature flags in your starter kit to allow clients to toggle features without redeploying the entire application.
Pre-configure monitoring and error logging (like Sentry) in your starter kit so you are alerted to issues before your client is.
Always build your database schemas with growth in mind; use UUIDs instead of integer IDs to prevent enumeration attacks and scaling bottlenecks.
content.next_steps: After completing your setup, run a full integration test on your authentication flow and deploy your first preview environment to ensure your pipeline is fully operational.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for basic apps and start building high-value, scalable SaaS products that dominate the market. Your expertise deserves a professional-grade foundation that works as hard as you do. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
